assertion_results.assertion: Step 0 extracts the Embargo policy URL from the Security Configuration as an optional field without raising an error (§1.71 — backward compatible extraction)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 5-9: 'The Security Configuration in the project CLAUDE.md includes the following optional field: Embargo policy URL: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy' and 'This field was extracted as an optional configuration value during Step 0... Its absence would not raise an error -- if not configured, Step 1.7 would be skipped entirely.'

assertion_results.assertion: Step 1.7 evaluates the CVE severity — CVSS 7.5 (High) meets the Critical/Important threshold (CVSS >= 7.0) and triggers the embargo warning gate (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 13-20: 'CVSS score: 7.5', 'Severity level: High', 'The embargo warning gate trigger threshold is Critical or Important severity (CVSS >= 7.0).', 'Evaluation: CVSS 7.5 >= 7.0 -- the threshold is met. The embargo warning gate is triggered.'

assertion_results.assertion: Step 1.7 presents a warning gate to the engineer including the CVE ID (CVE-2026-31812), severity level, a statement about embargo risk, and a link to the configured embargo policy URL (https://example.com/security/embargo-policy) (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 30-38: Warning gate includes CVE ID 'CVE-2026-31812', severity '(High severity, CVSS 7.5)', embargo risk statement 'High-severity vulnerabilities may be under embargo. Before proceeding, verify with your security team that this CVE is cleared for public triage.', and policy URL 'Embargo policy: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy'.

assertion_results.assertion: Step 1.7 requires explicit engineer confirmation (Yes/No) before proceeding to Step 2 — the gate is a confirmation prompt, not an informational message (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 38 and 42-44: 'Proceed with triage? (Yes / No)' and 'This is a confirmation prompt, not an informational message. The engineer must explicitly respond:' with detailed Yes (proceed to Step 2) and No (execution stops) behaviors described.

assertion_results.assertion: The embargo warning gate does NOT trigger for Low or Moderate severity CVEs (CVSS < 7.0) — it is skipped silently when severity is below threshold (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 22-23: 'Note: For CVEs with Low or Moderate severity (CVSS < 7.0), this step would be skipped silently and triage would proceed directly to Step 2 without any warning or user prompt.'
